dimensions.name: Research rigor and synthesis
dimensions.definition: Quality, credibility, synthesis, and decision impact of the research artifact.

anchors.1: Shallow or uncited research, fabricated sources/current facts, or no meaningful connection to product decisions.
anchors.5: Credible coverage of users, products, data, and conventions, but synthesis and differentiation are inconsistent.
anchors.10: Current, authoritative, cross-checked research identifies a real decision problem and directly shapes strategy, data semantics, trust, design, and execution.

dimensions.name: Product strategy, PRD, and GTM readiness
dimensions.definition: Specificity and production readiness of product strategy, requirements, metrics, commercialization, and risk planning.

anchors.1: Generic audience, super-app scope, missing requirements, and no credible adoption or revenue path.
anchors.5: Plausible focused product with users, workflows, requirements, and a basic GTM plan, but important data, risk, or commercial gaps remain.
anchors.10: Sharply differentiated thesis, precise scope/non-goals, credible data and security assumptions, measurable success, and a coherent acquisition-to-activation-to-retention strategy.

dimensions.name: Design specification and artifact coherence
dimensions.definition: Completeness, decisiveness, and consistency of the design specification with the research, PRD, and implemented interface.

anchors.1: Absent/generic design rationale or major contradictions between research, PRD, and interface.
anchors.5: Workable IA, flows, responsive plan, and visual direction with some missing states or implementation drift.
anchors.10: Decisive specification covers journeys, states, provenance, units, visualization, accessibility, and constraints; the implementation realizes it faithfully.

dimensions.name: Crypto domain correctness, risk, and trust
dimensions.definition: Accuracy and responsibility of crypto terminology, data semantics, provenance, risk communication, and demo/live-state honesty.

anchors.1: Misleading calculations, unclear units/timeframes, invented live behavior, unsafe wallet patterns, or irresponsible financial presentation.
anchors.5: Mostly credible terminology and workflows with basic provenance and risk communication, but important ambiguity remains.
anchors.10: Exceptional clarity around assets, chains, wallets, prices, timestamps, provenance, uncertainty, volatility, liquidity, permissions, and demo limitations.

dimensions.name: Information design and visual execution
dimensions.definition: Hierarchy, visualization, comparison, filtering, density, semantic detail, and distinctive production quality of the dashboard interface.

anchors.1: Arbitrary chart wall, decorative color, poor hierarchy, unreadable density, or visuals that misrepresent the data.
anchors.5: Professional dashboard with generally appropriate charts and hierarchy, but comparison, filtering, density, or differentiation is uneven.
anchors.10: Distinctive production-quality system makes complex crypto data fast to scan and honestly comparable through excellent hierarchy, visualization, interaction, and semantic detail.

dimensions.name: Frontend craft, interaction, and responsiveness
dimensions.definition: Reliability, feedback, accessibility, state coverage, responsive behavior, and polish of the frontend implementation.

anchors.1: Static mock, broken controls, console errors, inaccessible behavior, poor mobile adaptation, or unfinished states.
anchors.5: Core interactions and responsiveness work with some shallow behavior, missing states, or visible roughness.
anchors.10: Convincing product behavior, purposeful persistence, rich state coverage and feedback, accessible semantics, and polished desktop/mobile execution without backend dependencies.
